session.user:
Search for test files that test the `pr list` and `dash` commands and may be failing because they use `("erk-pr",)` as default labels. Look at: 1. tests/commands/pr/test_list.py - search for label references like "erk-pr" or "erk-core" 2. tests/commands/test_dash_workflow_runs.py - search for label references
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
Return the exact lines that need to change and why the tests are failing (is it that the fake data has "erk-pr" labels but the query now searches for "erk-core"?).
session.assistant: I'll help you search for test files that may be failing due to label changes. Let me start by checking the documentation, then explore the relevant test files.

session.assistant: Good! I found references to label-related documentation. Let me read the label scheme document:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: PR and Plan Label Assignment Scheme 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding how plans, code PRs, and learn issues are labeled" 5→ - "working with erk-pr, erk-plan, erk-core, or erk-learn labels" 6→ - "debugging label filtering in TUI or CLI views" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "querying core erk PRs without using the erk-core label" 9→ warning: "Use erk-core to query non-learn PRs (plans + code). Use erk-pr for ALL erk-submitted PRs including learn. Use type-specific labels (erk-plan, erk-learn) only when you need to filter to a specific type." 10→ - action: "backfilling labels on existing issues without considering updated_at side effects" 11→ warning: "GitHub label operations change the issue's updated_at timestamp. This affects sort order in list views and may confuse users." 12→--- 13→ 14→# PR and Plan Label Assignment Scheme 15→ 16→Erk uses `erk-pr` as the **universal base label** on all erk-submitted PRs, with `erk-core` for non-learn PRs and **type-specific labels** for filtering. 17→ 18→## Label Taxonomy 19→ 20→| Label | Purpose | Applied To | 21→| ------------------ | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- | 22→| `erk-pr` | Base label — all erk-submitted PRs | All plans, learn plans, and code PRs | 23→| `erk-core` | Core work label — non-learn PRs | Plan PRs and code PRs (NOT learn PRs) | 24→| `erk-planned-pr` | Legacy base label — identifies plans | Historical plans only (no longer applied by code) | 25→| `erk-plan` | Type label — implementation plans | Regular plans | 26→| `erk-learn` | Type label — documentation plans | Learn plans | 27→| `erk-objective` | Separate system — objectives | Objective issues | 28→| `erk-consolidated` | Consolidation marker | Consolidated learn plans | 29→ 30→## Label Assignment
... [49 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: CI Label Rename Checklist 3→read_when: 4→ - renaming a GitHub label used in CI automation 5→ - updating label references across the codebase 6→ - debugging why CI label checks aren't working after a rename 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "Renaming a GitHub label used in CI automation" 9→ warning: "Labels are referenced in multiple places: (1) Job-level if: conditions in all workflow files, (2) Step name descriptions and comments, (3) Documentation examples showing the label check. Missing any location will cause CI behavior to diverge from intent. Use the CI Label Rename Checklist to ensure comprehensive updates." 10→ score: 6 11→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 12→audit_result: clean 13→--- 14→ 15→# CI Label Rename Checklist 16→ 17→## Why Label Renames Are Dangerous 18→ 19→GitHub Actions label checks use **string literals in YAML that cannot be validated statically**. Unlike code references that fail at compile time, label mismatches fail silently: 20→ 21→- Boolean expressions evaluate to `false` when label names don't match 22→- No errors are thrown at workflow parse time or runtime 23→- PRs with the label run CI when they should skip (or vice versa) 24→- No obvious indicators point to the mismatch 25→ 26→The problem: **labels connect Python constants, YAML workflow conditions, and prose documentation across three different file formats with zero static validation**. 27→ 28→## The Coordination Challenge 29→ 30→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/constants.py, PLAN_REVIEW_LABEL -->
... [94 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Service for efficiently fetching plan list data via batched API calls. 2→ 3→Uses GraphQL nodes(ids: [...]) for O(1) batch lookup of workflow runs (~200ms for any N). 4→All plans store last_dispatched_node_id in the plan-header metadata block. 5→ 6→Performance optimization: When PR linkages are needed, uses unified GraphQL query via 7→get_issues_with_pr_linkages() to fetch issues + PR linkages in a single API call (~600ms), 8→instead of separate calls for issues (~500ms) and PR linkages (~1500ms). 9→""" 10→ 11→from __future__ import annotations 12→ 13→import logging 14→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any 15→ 16→from erk_shared.core.plan_list_service import PlanListData as PlanListData 17→from erk_shared.core.plan_list_service import PlanListService 18→from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import LocalGitHub 19→from erk_shared.gateway.github.graphql_queries import GET_WORKFLOW_RUNS_BY_NODE_IDS_QUERY 20→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 21→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import extract_plan_header_dispatch_info 22→from erk_shared.gateway.github.pr_data_parsing import ( 23→ merge_rest_graphql_pr_data, 24→ parse_workflow_runs_nodes_response, 25→) 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import ( 27→ GitHubRepoLocation, 28→ IssueFilterState, 29→ WorkflowRun, 30→)
... [408 lines omitted] ...
177→ warnings = _build_enrichment_warnings(unenriched_count, len(pr_details_list)) 186→ warnings=warnings, 265→ except Exception as e: 266→ logging.warning("Failed to fetch workflow runs via HTTP: %s", e) 308→ except RuntimeError as e: 309→ logging.warning("Failed to fetch workflow runs: %s", e) 313→def _build_enrichment_warnings(unenriched_count: int, total_count: int) -> tuple[str, ...]: 314→ """Build warning messages for degraded GraphQL enrichment data. 321→ Tuple of warning strings (empty if no warnings) 326→ f"GraphQL enrichment failed for {unenriched_count}/{total_count} PRs " 415→ except Exception as e: 416→ # Network/API failure - continue without workflow run data 418→ logging.warning("Failed to fetch workflow runs: %s", e)

1→"""Serialize plan dashboard data to JSON for external consumers. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec dash-data [--state open|closed] [--label LABEL] [--limit N] 5→ [--show-prs/--no-show-prs] [--show-runs/--no-show-runs] 6→ [--run-state STATE] [--creator USER] 7→ 8→Output: 9→ JSON with {success, plans, count} 10→ 11→Exit Codes: 12→ 0: Success 13→ 1: Error (missing repo info, API error) 14→""" 15→ 16→import json 17→ 18→import click 19→ 20→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 21→from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir 22→from erk.tui.data.real_provider import RealPlanDataProvider 23→from erk.tui.data.types import PlanFilters, serialize_plan_row 24→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_context 25→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId, GitHubRepoLocation, IssueFilterState 26→ 27→ 28→@click.command(name="dash-data") 29→@click.option("--state", type=click.Choice(["open", "closed"]), default=None) 30→@click.option("--label", multiple=True, default=("erk-core",))

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report:
## Analysis Report: Test Files with Label Mismatch
I've found the issue causing the test failures. The code has changed from querying with **"erk-pr"** (the universal base label) to querying with **"erk-core"** (the non-learn label), but the test fake data hasn't been updated accordingly.
### Documentation Context According to `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/planning/label-scheme.md`, the new label scheme is: - **`erk-pr`**: Universal base label applied to ALL erk-submitted PRs (plans + learn plans + code PRs) - **`erk-core`**: Core work label for non-learn PRs only (plans + code PRs, NOT learn PRs) - **`erk-plan`**: Type-specific label for regular implementation plans - **`erk-learn`**: Type-specific label for learn plans
### The Issue: Default Labels Changed
**Files Affected:**
1. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py`** (lines 281, 400) - Line 281: `labels = label if label else ("erk-core",)` - Line 400: `labels = label if label else ("erk-core",)` - **Changed from**: Default should have been `"erk-pr"` to query all plans - **Changed to**: Now defaults to `"erk-core"` to query only non-learn plans
2. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/dash_data.py`** (line 30) - Line 30: `@click.option("--label", multiple=True, default=("erk-core",))` - **Changed to**: Defaults to `"erk-core"` instead of `"erk-pr"`
### Test Files with Failing Fake Data
**`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_list.py`**
This file creates Plan objects with hardcoded label tuples. All 12 test functions use: - Line 43: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 56: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 89: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 102: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 137: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan", "erk-queue"]` - Line 150: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 195: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 230: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 275: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 321: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 334: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 363: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]`
**Why tests fail:** The code now queries for `erk-core` label, but the fake Plan data only has `["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` labels. Since `erk-core` label is not present in the fake data, the label filter returns zero results.
**`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/test_dash_workflow_runs.py`**
This file also has the same pattern across multiple test functions: - Line 85: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 181: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 281: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 363: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 439: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 550: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 564: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]` - Line 636: `labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"]`
Same issue: fake data has `erk-pr` but query is for `erk-core`.
### Lines That Need to Change
All fake Plan objects in both test files need to have `"erk-core"` added to their labels tuple:
**Before:** ```python labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"] ```
**After:** ```python labels=["erk-pr", "erk-core", "erk-plan"] ```
This change needs to be applied to every Plan instantiation in both test files to match the new query behavior where the `pr list` and `dash` commands default to filtering by `erk-core` label.
